executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated to MAJOR threat level following the US-Israeli preventive strike on Iran (Feb 28) and reported killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei. Iran has responded with ballistic missile barrages against Israel and strikes on 14 US bases across Iraq, Syria, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Qatar. The IRGC Navy attacked US naval vessels, and Gulf states (Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, UAE) are actively intercepting Iranian missiles and drones. Regional airspace is closed, aviation disrupted, and the conflict is spreading beyond traditional battlefields with civilian casualties mounting.
whats_new: Bahrain announces interception of new wave of Iranian ballistic missiles and drones (March 1)
Qatari fighter jets and air defense systems intercept Iranian aerial attacks targeting Qatari airspace
Explosions reported in northwest Tehran's Saadat Abad area (4 explosions)
Iranian drones target Omani port (Port of Duqm), injuring one person
Two missiles launched towards Cyprus where thousands of British troops are stationed
Death toll from Iran school strike rises to 108 (Iran Red Crescent)
UAE air defenses intercept drone; debris injures two in Abu Dhabi residential area
Oil tanker attacked off Oman coast near Strait of Hormuz, four injured
confirmed_events: US and Israel launched coordinated preventive airstrike on Iran targeting government facilities and IRGC centers (Feb 28, 06:16 UTC) — confirmed by multiple sources including US/Israeli media
Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei reportedly killed in Israeli airstrikes on Tehran — reported by Jordan Times, Washington Post, Daily Sabah, The Independent
Iran launched ballistic missile barrages against Israel (Haifa, Galilee region) — confirmed by multiple sources
Iran struck 14 US military bases across Iraq, Syria, and Gulf region with ballistic missiles and drones — IRGC claims responsibility, multiple source corroboration
IRGC Navy launched missile attacks on US MST support ship — multiple reports confirm direct naval engagement
Iranian missile strikes on US base in Erbil, Iraq — confirmed by regional sources
Explosions occurred in eastern Riyadh, Saudi Arabia — reported by RBC/Al Jazeera
Gulf states (Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, UAE) intercept Iranian missiles/drones — confirmed by national news agencies (QNA, TASS)
At least 85 children killed in school strike in southern Iran (Minab) — Iran Red Crescent reports 108 dead
Kuwaiti air defense systems intercepted multiple targets in southern Kuwait — TASS
Gulf countries closed airspace due to Middle East tensions — TASS
Houthis (Ansar Allah) pledge full support to Iran, commit to 'promised conquest' — Al Alam
Hezbollah Leader condemns death of Khamenei, calls him martyr — Hezbollah Electronic Army
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims 200 US military personnel killed in Iranian strikes on American bases — IRGC claim, unverifed casualty figure
Dozens of top Iranian regime officials killed alongside Supreme Leader in Israeli strikes — Jerusalem Post analysis, unconfirmed details
Iranian missiles and suicide drone attacks on Dubai targeting Western tourists — South China Morning Post reports, targeting details unconfirmed
Four explosions in Tehran's Saadat Abad district — War Monitors report, no official confirmation
Iranian drone attack on Port of Duqm in Oman — Middle East Eye, limited corroboration
British influencer recounts missile strike near Dubai hotel — Times of India, personal account
London street celebrations over Khamenei's death — Daily Mail report, unverifiable crowd size/claims

people_travel: At least 27 flights from Hong Kong to Middle East (Riyadh, Doha, Dubai) cancelled or delayed
Gulf airspace closure impacting regional and international routes
CBSE postpones Class 10 and 12 board exams in Middle East for Indian students
Airline disruption expected to continue as conflict escalates
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz under threat — oil tanker attacked off Oman's Musandam Governorate
Port of Duqm in Oman targeted by Iranian drones, disrupting regional logistics
UAE air defenses active, creating delays in maritime operations near Abu Dhabi
Supply routes through Gulf states face increased risk as conflict expands
market_macro: Oil prices likely to spike given Strait of Hormuz threat and tanker attacks
Regional stock markets under pressure as conflict spreads to Gulf capitals
Aviation sector facing significant disruption costs with flight cancellations
Energy infrastructure risk premium increasing with each Iranian missile launch toward Gulf states

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is expected to intensify over the next 24-72 hours. Iran has announced 'more severe retaliatory measures' and IRGC vows continued strikes on US assets. With Khamenei's death confirmed by multiple sources, Iran faces a leadership vacuum but military response is likely to accelerate rather than diminish. Gulf states (Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, UAE) will remain high-value targets for Iranian retaliation. The US-Israeli achievement of 'air supremacy over Iran' (1,200 bombs dropped) provides operational dominance but may provoke more asymmetric responses from IRGC and proxies.
escalation_risks: Iranian attacks on Gulf state infrastructure expanding — Qatar and Bahrain now directly targeted
Missiles toward Cyprus indicate conflict spreading to NATO assets, potentially drawing UK into direct hostilities
Hezbollah and Houthi proxies likely to escalate attacks on Israel and Saudi Arabia/US allies
Civilian casualty escalation (schools, residential areas) increases international pressure andIranian domestic rallying effect
Potential forIranian cyberattacks on Gulf state infrastructure or US financial systems
Unverified reports of Tehran explosions suggest possible internal instability or Israeli intelligence operations
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: UN Secretary-General has urged all parties to 'see reason' but diplomatic channels appear closed. Iran views the killing of Khamenei as a red line making de-escalation unlikely without major concessions. The US has stated ally safety is 'unconditional,' signaling continued support for Israeli operations. Any de-escalation would require either: (1) Iran accepting significant losses and ceasing retaliation, (2) US-Israel agreeing to pause operations amid leadership transition, or (3) external pressure from Russia/China or Gulf states willing to mediate. None of these appear imminent as of current reporting.
